data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. SDK-style implicit globs expand at *evaluation* time (before targets run), so files created during the build aren't automatically picked up by the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` (and `FileWrites` for clean support) within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it (globs expand at evaluation time, before this target runs) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before any targets run), so files created during the build aren't automatically picked up by the implicit `Compile` glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also to `FileWrites` for proper clean support.

This is the classic "including-generated-files" problem. The target needs to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group so the compiler knows about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it (globs expand at evaluation time, before this target runs) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the summary:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` during the build, but SDK-style implicit globs (`<Compile Include="**/*.cs" />`) expand at *evaluation* time — before any targets execute. The generated file doesn't exist yet when globs are evaluated, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Explicitly add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target (at execution time), along with `FileWrites` so `dotnet clean` removes it:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```
